title: Process guardrail: prevent reactive-cascade / house-of-cards fix accretion
description:
2026-07-07/08 session: agent responded to a runner-fleet incident with a cascade of reactive point-fixes -- deployed po2 respawn-pacing gate to stop watchdog reboots, then relaxed the watchdog (removing po2's reason to exist), then po2's stale hardcoded ceiling (validation rejects >=24.0, tied to the OLD watchdog max-load-1=24) throttled fleet recovery even after the watchdog was raised to 96. The gap was correctly identified and captured as bead ez-gh-actions-rfk but filed as P2 "tune later" instead of treated as an urgent same-breath follow-up, so the fleet ran needlessly throttled. Net effect: a daemon that originally had zero respawn throttles accreted ~69 lines of interacting load-gate/ceiling/batch/budget logic in one day, and reactive fixes made the system more fragile, not less. Also involved repeated mis-diagnosis under pressure (declared po2 deployed-and-working while fleet was actually draining; blamed po2, then found GH rate limit; blamed budget, then found secondary rate limit) and premature success declarations without ground-truth verification.

Tracking bead for applying the harness fixes recommended by the /harness meta-analysis (see conversation for full 5-Whys + proposed rule text): a "second-fix-means-stop-and-reassess" rule, an "audit and delete obsolete workarounds when you fix a root cause" rule, a "verify with ground truth before declaring success" rule, and a complexity-budget self-check for incident-pressure changes. Proposed homes: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (global agent-behavior rule, not repo-specific), a Claude memory entry, and a self-invoked checklist for 3rd+ consecutive fix to the same subsystem.

title: fix(src/main.rs): wait_for_backend in-process retry around choose_backend
description: Currently src/main.rs:201-220 (Commands::Serve arm) calls choose_backend(cfg)?; — if it returns Err (e.g. Selection::None because lima isn't up yet), main() exits code 1, systemd restarts at RestartSec=10. The Jul 6 incident produced 1381 of these. Replace the immediate ? with wait_for_backend(cfg)?; which loops with capped exponential backoff (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 300s-cap) and only returns Ok once choose_backend succeeds. Once the daemon is in its main 30s loop, transient docker-socket blips are already contained; this change specifically protects the cold-start path. Concrete Rust patch in advisor review Q7. Verify with cargo test, then rebuild + restart per Gate 0.
notes: Implemented a9a7ab1: wait_for_backend() retries backend::select() for up to 120s, sleeping 5s between attempts. serve subcommand uses this instead of choose_backend().

title: REST+GraphQL bucket split for gh API calls -- fix registration starvation
description:
ROOT CAUSE (confirmed via journal 2026-07-07 22:47-22:51): github.rs routes ALL
calls through `gh api` (REST). REST and GraphQL are separate 5000/hr rate-limit
buckets. Heavy read-side monitoring (queue_monitor's queued-run enumeration,
per-run job listing, invariant_sampler's runner/job polling) exhausts the REST
bucket, starving the critical write path (generate_jitconfig / runner
registration, also REST) -- ephemeral runners can't register, exit immediately,
fleet bleeds toward 0 even though load/po2 are fine.

FIX:
1. Move READ-HEAVY monitoring to GraphQL: queue_monitor's queued-run + per-run
   job enumeration, and runner status listing. One GraphQL query replaces many
   REST calls -- more efficient AND a separate quota bucket.
2. KEEP registration/mutations on REST: generate_jitconfig has no GraphQL
   equivalent, must stay REST.
3. Add bucket-aware fallback: on a 403 in one transport, call `gh api
   rate_limit` (returns both buckets' remaining) and route to whichever bucket
   has budget; log which bucket is exhausted.

PROCESS (per user directive 2026-07-07): implement via codex (zero Claude
quota, Claude 429 pressure), small Claude swarm (2-3 agents max) for
adversarial review only, not a big fan-out. Branch: sidekick/rest-graphql-split.
TDD. Commit+push after every green unit of work, never hold >30min
uncommitted. Do NOT merge/deploy without main's review + a low-load window.

title: fix(lima-vm@colima.service): Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes + drop --foreground + TimeoutSec=300
description: Current unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/lima-vm@colima.service has Type=simple + TimeoutSec=10 and runs 'limactl start colima --foreground' in the foreground. On warm boot (VM already running) this completes in 16ms and seems fine; on cold boot, limactl needs minutes to bring up the QEMU guest, gets SIGKILLed by TimeoutSec=10, and the unit fails. Verified empirically with 'limactl start colima' on running VM (16ms 'Using the existing instance') and watching the cold-boot get killed. Fix unit to: drop --foreground (limactl daemonizes itself, returns when VM is SSH-able), Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes (so unit is 'active' after limactl returns, until asked to stop), TimeoutSec=10 → 300 (bound worst-case cold-boot cloud-init). Type=notify is NOT viable because limactl doesn't emit sd_notify. This single change is the highest leverage in the fix set; without it, no consumer-side Requires= will work on cold-boot.
notes: Implemented f202638: lima-vm@colima.service patched to Type=forking, drop --foreground, RemainAfterExit=yes, TimeoutStartSec=300, Restart=no, ExecStop=. Deployed + checked in to docs/lima-vm-colima.service.

title: Design periodic GitHub Actions stuck-run reaper
notes: Design target: add a periodic, dry-run-first stuck-run reaper for jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai and other QUEUE_REPO values. It must be job-aware: do not classify a run as stale only by workflow_run.created_at because reruns/current attempts can have old run.created_at but new job.started_at/run_started_at. Proposed policy: every 10-15 minutes, scan queued and in_progress runs with REST; fetch jobs for candidates; compute current_attempt_age from max(job.started_at, run.run_started_at, run.updated_at) for queued jobs. Actions: (1) cancel-then-delete only queued run objects whose run.created_at > 8h AND current_attempt_age > 60-90m, (2) cancel superseded queued runs only when a newer queued or in_progress run exists for same repo/workflow/branch and candidate current_attempt_age > 20-30m, (3) never touch in_progress by default; report in_progress jobs over policy separately with runner/job URL, (4) label-mismatch detector reports queued jobs whose labels have no matching online runner labels, e.g. agent-orchestrator or warp-*; it does not cancel them. Install path: systemd user timer on Linux host using ~/.cargo/bin/ezgha or scripts/reap-stuck-runs.sh, with log file under ~/.local/state/ezgha/reaper.jsonl and alert() on mutations/failures. Safety: dry-run default, --apply only for timer after config enables it, max mutations per run, REST rate-limit floor, lock file, exact URLs in logs, no tail cancellation without explicit allowlist.

Deliverable = durable guardrails: (a) CLAUDE.md 'blast-radius / interaction review' rule for ANY change to a threshold/health-check/watchdog/restart-policy/resource-limit/monitor-cadence, requiring the change to state the normal peak of the bounded metric and the remaining margin; (b) principle 'a safety/health/monitoring mechanism must not be able to cause the failure it guards against'; (c) doctor.sh gate flagging watchdog max-load-1 vs observed respawn peak margin; (d) version-control /etc/watchdog.conf + margin rationale in-repo. title: graceful daemon re-exec (ezgha reload) — zero-respawn-wave code rollouts
description:
User's idea (2026-07-07, via main during po2 deploy). Currently every ezgha
daemon code update requires systemctl --user restart ezgha.service, which
kills and respawns the daemon process -- runner containers survive (separate
Docker procs) but the daemon re-adopts them cold via ensure_count on the new
process's first tick, and if timed wrong (load>12, containers<12) this has
tripped the host watchdog reboot twice in one day (2026-07-07).

Proposed: an `ezgha reload` command/signal that does an in-place exec() of
the new binary between serve-loop ticks (like nginx/systemd graceful reload),
re-adopting the already-running containers via the existing slot_assignments
+ ensure_count reconciliation path rather than a full process kill+restart.
Combined with the po2 gate-primary respawn pacing (merged 2026-07-07,
commit d612ad7/deployed 6a3138d), this would eliminate the restart-driven
reboot risk entirely -- code rollouts would never need the careful-restart
load<12/containers>=12 gate because there's no respawn wave to pace, just a
process re-exec with the existing container fleet immediately re-adopted.

Do NOT implement yet -- P2, capture only. Implementation routes through
/sidekick (sidekick3) or a swarm when picked up, not direct main coding,
per the 2026-07-07 delegation directive.

title: Pace runner respawns to avoid tripping host watchdog(8) reboot
description: ensure_count/docker_backend currently can respawn many runner slots simultaneously (JIT + docker run per slot), which spikes 1-min load average enough to trip /etc/watchdog.conf's max-load-1=24 threshold on this 32-thread box -- confirmed root cause of two host reboots today (00:48 loadavg 71 on a full cold respawn of 16; 12:50 loadavg 25-28), the second of which killed the runner-truly-healthy mission's predecessor sidekick session outright. Operator/session-level discipline (checking uptime before every systemctl restart) is not durable -- it dies with the session. Fix: add bounded concurrency to ensure_count (e.g. max 3-4 simultaneous docker run + JIT registration calls in flight, batched with a short sleep between batches) and/or a load-aware backoff (check /proc/loadavg or getloadavg() before spawning a batch, delay if already elevated) in docker_backend.rs, so the daemon is safe to restart by ANY operator at ANY time regardless of how many slots need respawning at once. This also directly serves the E1/INV-1 utilization invariant: gentler respawn waves mean shallower busy-count dips, which should show up as fewer inv1_fail_class=offline-respawning samples in invariant_history.jsonl once this lands -- that data itself is a validation signal for the fix. Coordinate scope with the parallel Jeff-Ubuntu crash-investigation sidekick's staggered-respawn ask (goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/04-coordination-crash-mission.md) to avoid duplicating work. Also feeds E4's ed8 fleet-churn root-cause requirement (docs/ed8-fleet-churn-root-cause-20260707.md).

title: Cap E1 invariant sampler job-enumeration cost + share fetch with queue_monitor
description: The E1 daemon-native invariant sampler (src/queue_monitor.rs, InvariantSamplerState/sample_invariants) currently calls fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet per monitored repo, which loops over EVERY queued run and calls gh api list_workflow_jobs once per run to get job-level data. With queued_jobs=1290 measured live, a single tick can take minutes of sequential API calls (observed: alert fired 263s after the sample's own ts), which likely contributes to the GitHub secondary rate limit already affecting Gate 4. Approved fix (main's design, 2026-07-07 14:xx PT), scope: (a) cap per-tick job enumeration to the OLDEST ~50 queued runs per repo -- INV-2 stays exact since the oldest job necessarily lives among the oldest runs (sort by created_at ascending, take first 50 before the per-run job fetch loop); add a queued_jobs_capped:bool field to the InvariantSample schema, true whenever a repo had more than 50 queued runs at fetch time, so the JSONL honestly signals queued_jobs is a lower bound rather than an exact count in that case. (b) share ONE fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet result per repo per daemon tick between QueueMonitorState::maybe_check and InvariantSamplerState::maybe_sample instead of each independently re-fetching the same worldarchitect.ai data -- likely requires restructuring main.rs's serve loop to fetch once and pass the snapshot(s) to both tick handlers, or introducing a small per-tick snapshot cache keyed by repo with a short TTL. Update/add unit tests for the cap logic (e.g. 60 queued jobs input -> only oldest 50 considered, queued_jobs_capped=true; 30 input -> all considered, queued_jobs_capped=false) and for the shared-fetch behavior if a cache is introduced. Coordinate with bead ez-gh-actions-po2 (respawn pacing) -- both target the same underlying GitHub API rate-limit pressure from different angles (this bead reduces sampler-induced API load; po2 reduces the docker-run/JIT-registration burst that trips the host watchdog) and both should be dispatched to codex together once quota returns (~14:52 PT 2026-07-07).

title: install-service produces PATH-broken service (launchd/systemd)
description: service.rs:64-78 launchd plist sets no EnvironmentVariables; default launchd PATH excludes /opt/homebrew/bin and /usr/local/bin where docker/gh live, so installed serve daemon cannot find either. launchctl load -w also bails if already loaded (re-run breaks). Linux: After=docker.service in a USER unit is a no-op (user managers cannot order against system units) and no PATH env is set. Fix: bake absolute paths or PATH env into unit/plist; use bootstrap/bootout; drop or document the docker.service ordering.

title: fix(ezgha.service): Restart=always + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600
description: Base unit currently has 'Restart=on-failure' + 'RestartSec=10'. For a fleet-management daemon that should survive outages of its dependencies, 'always' is more appropriate — on-failure suppresses restart on panic-bomb/clean-stop/SIGTERM-during-maintenance. With RestartSec=15 + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600, the 6th restart happens ~10min after the 1st (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 600s-cap), well past any lima cold-boot. systemd 255 (verified installed) supports all three knobs. Edit ~/.config/systemd/user/ezgha.service base file generated by src/service.rs.
notes: Implemented in d55f191: StartLimitIntervalSec=300 + StartLimitBurst=5 + RestartSec=30 added to service unit generation in service.rs. Restart=on-failure kept (Restart=always would fight with lima dep).

title: BUG: free_disk_gb guard measures Colima VM overlay, not host FS (docker_backend.rs:761-780)
description: Swarm adversarial review 2026-07-07 (coldstart, confirmed against real code). ezgha's disk-floor guard free_disk_gb runs 'docker run --entrypoint df' (src/docker_backend.rs:761-780) which measures the Docker/Colima VM overlay filesystem, NOT the macOS/Linux HOST filesystem. Impact TODAY (independent of cold-start): the disk-floor protection can read healthy while the host disk (where ~/.config/ezgha, images, logs, and any future mirror cache live) is critically low, or vice-versa — the guard may be protecting the wrong filesystem. Hypotheses to investigate: (1) they predate the force-cancel-fallback deploy (commit 071ce2e) and the plain-cancel 202-but-stays-queued behavior left them wedged, and subsequent ticks' batched re-listing force-cancel only targets ids IT cancelled that tick, NOT pre-existing wedged runs from prior ticks; (2) the per-page=100 listing may not include them if queue>100 and they sort late. LIKELY ROOT CAUSE = #1: the batched force-cancel only re-checks runs cancelled in the SAME tick; a run wedged in a PRIOR tick is re-plain-cancelled (202, no-op) forever, never force-cancelled. FIX: in the tail pass, force-cancel ANY candidate still queued after its plain cancel regardless of which tick first cancelled it — i.e., track/force-cancel by age>threshold+still-queued, not by same-tick membership. Repro: any run that survives one throttled/failed tick. Ties to jleechan-me9 (reaper hardening).

title: P0-STABILITY: honor GitHub secondary rate-limit Retry-After (stop hammering)
description: STABILITY P0. Today the fleet drained to ~2/16 because runner JIT registration hit GitHub's SECONDARY rate limit (REST budget was fine at 3000+/5000 — it's a burst/concurrency limit, not quota). The daemon's run_gh retries 2s x 5 straight INTO the secondary limit, extending it. Fix (github.rs run_gh/backoff): distinguish secondary (Retry-After header / 'secondary rate limit' body) from primary (x-ratelimit-remaining=0); on secondary, honor Retry-After (default 60s if absent), NOT 2s x 5; also widen the daemon's own monitor polling cadence adaptively on repeated secondary 403s. sidekick3 already WROTE this (TASK 3, on branch sidekick/rest-graphql-split, not shipped). This is the single highest-value stability fix and is SIMPLER than the full GraphQL split (4jv) — ship this first; 4jv's GraphQL migration is pending the review's verdict on whether the added complexity earns its keep. Pairs with zai (double-fetch).

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: RESEARCH (cited): GitHub docs mandate for SECONDARY limits: honor Retry-After; if absent wait >=60s then EXPONENTIAL backoff (not fixed 2s); pause >=1s between mutating calls; repeated fast retries 'may result in banning of your integration'. Our 2s x5 is a direct violation, plausibly the DIRECT cause of the drain. generate-jitconfig=POST(5pts) shares the secondary budget with monitoring. ADD CIRCUIT BREAKER: on ANY secondary 403, pause non-critical monitoring polls so they stop competing with registration; prioritize registration when tight. Precedent: actions-runner-controller shipped exp backoff for registration (v0.10.0) for this exact thundering-herd. Secondary caps: <=100 concurrent, 900pts/min REST.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: CORRECTION (verified in code): run_gh_with_backoff ALREADY honors Retry-After via max(retry_after, exponential), 5 attempts. So 'add Retry-After' is DONE — this bead's real remaining gaps are NARROWER: (1) GH_RETRY_MAX_DELAY caps at 32s but GitHub's floor for secondary-limit-without-header is >=60s — raise the cap / add a secondary-specific >=60s floor; (2) CIRCUIT BREAKER to pause monitoring polls on any secondary 403 (still missing). BUT per advice-p0: the daemon's own timers are the SMALL contributor. The dominant load is EXTERNAL — N agents across 2 machines on one token. The real fix is governance (see new deploy-serialization + GitHub App nuk beads), not daemon polling hygiene. Demote this to P2.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: User-requested emphasis 2026-07-08: this is the retry-after / secondary-limit backoff fix. Retry-After parsing EXISTS in run_gh_with_backoff but caps at GH_RETRY_MAX_DELAY=32s (below GitHub's >=60s secondary-limit floor) and there's no circuit-breaker. ACTIONABLE: (1) raise/branch the cap so secondary-limit waits honor >=60s + exponential; (2) circuit-breaker pausing monitoring polls on any secondary 403 so they stop competing with runner registration; (3) distinguish secondary (Retry-After/secondary-limit body) from primary. Bumped P2->P1 per user. Related: nuk (GitHub App isolated budget), le7 (fewer consumers).

title: P1-STABILITY: migrate ezgha to a GitHub App token (isolated, larger rate budget)
description: RESEARCH-driven root-cause fix. Today's rate-limit drain traces to MANY consumers (2 machines, N agents, daemon monitors + registration) sharing ONE PAT's secondary-limit budget. GitHub App: isolated budget, 5000->12500 req/hr scaling, separate from human/agent PAT usage. A real ARC user with 360 ephemeral runners hit secondary limits every ~20min on a shared PAT; moving to a GitHub App was step 1 of their fix (ARC discussion #1153). This isolates the daemon's registration/monitoring from all the other token consumers — addresses the ORGANIZATIONAL root cause technically. Bigger change (app registration, install, key mgmt) — scope + confirm with user. Pairs with yrt (backoff) + zai (consolidate).

title: P1-STABILITY: alert on registration-failure-rate + queue-time, not just online count
description: RESEARCH-driven monitoring honesty. Today's core failure: 'N runners online' LIED — runners were online-but-not-registering-new-jobs while the fleet was actually starved. GitHub docs + SRE practice: alert on (a) runner REGISTRATION FAILURE RATE (not current count), (b) JOB QUEUE TIME growth (GitHub auto-cancels queued jobs after 24h — sustained queue-time growth is the definitive 'fleet not accepting work' signal). Direction: prefer WEBHOOK-driven signals (workflow_job events) over polling — reduces API load AND is a truer health signal than polled counts. The E1 sampler already tracks queue tail; add a registration-failure-rate signal + a 'busy-but-not-accepting-jobs' detector. This is the honest-monitoring layer that would have caught today's drain in minutes instead of via user report.

title: P0-STABILITY: single-owner deploy lock — no concurrent Gate-0 builds/restarts on the fleet
description: THE real P0 per advice-p0 root-cause analysis. Today's mechanism of harm: N concurrent agent sessions across 2 machines each build+cargo-install+restart ezgha racing each other, with NO serialization. This is HOW unreviewed code reached prod (bead jleechan-qo8: Mac ran uncommitted ccbd10e-dirty, Linux 6a3138d, NEITHER on main — Gate 0 violated) and how the po2 pacing-deadlock bug went live. Arbitrary unreviewed code can be running on the fleet RIGHT NOW. FIX: a single-owner deploy lock (flock on a shared file / a designated deploy session) so only ONE session builds+deploys at a time, and Gate-0 refuses to deploy a non-main / dirty binary. Cross-machine: each host deploys only from origin/main HEAD, verified before restart. This is more load-bearing than any daemon fix — it's the change-control layer that would have prevented the whole day. Pairs with jleechan-qo8 (the bug it fixes) + gfl/2v8 (blast-radius review gate).

title: P0-STABILITY: dead-man's-switch — self-test the alert pipeline is actually alive

FIX: a periodic alert-pipeline heartbeat/self-test (e.g. daemon posts a low-noise 'alerting alive' canary on a schedule and/or asserts webhook reachability at startup + logs LOUDLY if the alert channel is unconfigured/unreachable). Also fold: ezgha.service uses Wants= (not Requires=) lima-vm@colima — it starts even if the VM fails -> 'no usable backend' with NO alert (verified). Add a startup alert-if-no-backend. Same SPOF category as the watchdog.conf gap (gfl) but for the monitoring layer itself.

title: Investigate: scope org runner group to worldarchitect.ai (stop cross-repo capacity bleed)
description: ROOT-CAUSE finding (2026-07-08, corroborated by GHA-mechanics research): org-level ez-* runners serve EVERY repo in jleechanorg, not just worldarchitect.ai. When worldai is saturated (117-163 queued), some of the ~20 runners may be executing OTHER org repos' jobs — invisible when filtering the Actions page to worldai, and a capacity bleed from worldai's backlog. DIAGNOSTIC (research step 3, cheap): collect busy runner_ids from /orgs/jleechanorg/actions/runners, then for each org repo's in_progress runs collect job runner_ids; any busy runner not running a worldai job is on another repo. IF significant cross-repo consumption: scope the runner group (Settings > Actions > Runner groups) to worldarchitect.ai only, OR add runners. NOTE: do the diagnostic when the secondary rate limit clears (API is currently truncating responses — every count is unreliable; use local docker top for fleet truth). Related: ksy (capacity decision), nuk (GitHub App observability), the local docker top proved 14/14 linux runners EXECUTING jobs (fleet is healthy/fully-utilized, no zombies).

title: doctor.sh sections 7/8 can hang indefinitely under sustained Actions-API rate limit (undocumented bucket, not shown by gh api rate_limit)

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: Discovered live during ez-gh-actions-g3o verification (2026-07-08): repos/{repo}/actions/runs/{id}/jobs returned {"message":"API rate limit exceeded for user ID ...","status":"403"} for 10+ minutes straight, while gh api rate_limit core bucket showed remaining=3569/5000 (not exhausted). This is a separate, undocumented Actions-API-specific throttle not exposed by any resource in the /rate_limit response. doctor.sh sections 7 (real job-execution proof) and 8 (queue-health.sh) both call this endpoint in a loop; under set -euo pipefail + process substitution, the script silently produces no further output for the remainder of the run once this bucket engages (observed: 3 separate full runs each stopped exactly at the section 7 header with 90s/300s/600s timeouts). Fix ideas: (1) wrap each per-run gh api call with a short timeout + explicit 403-rate-limit detection that skips remaining runs in the loop rather than hanging/silently truncating; (2) surface 'rate limited, N/6 runs checked' instead of going silent. Root-caused as external/orthogonal to the g3o fix -- section 9 (this session new per-slot proof) is immune since it is LOCAL-ONLY (docker top, no gh api calls).

Gate 0 should ignore docs/fmt-only HEAD advances (false-red on every non-functional commit)
description: Gate 0 in docs/verify-exit-criteria.sh compares deployed binary SHA to current git HEAD SHA verbatim, so ANY commit after a build (including pure docs or cargo-fmt-only commits) reds the gate even though nothing functional changed. Incident 2026-07-08: deployed the reaper zombie-slot self-heal + App-token fix (bfddf83/5f0374a) cleanly, then a STATE-mirror doc commit and a pure-whitespace cargo-fmt fixup each independently re-broke Gate 0, forcing 2 extra rebuild+restart cycles chasing SHA parity for zero functional benefit -- a textbook reactive-cascade trap (team-lead explicitly flagged this and told the deploy-owner to stop chasing it). Fix: Gate 0 should compare either (a) a source-tree hash that excludes docs/**, goals/**, *.md, and .beads/issues.jsonl, or (b) skip the check entirely when the only diff between deployed-SHA and HEAD is non-functional (docs/fmt-only), so cosmetic HEAD advances don't misreport a real deploy as broken. Tie to the 2026-07-08 reaper+App-token deploy incident; see goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/STATE-merge-driver.md for the full sequence.
